strategy: Preserve the known dialogue window, add speaker-attributed ASR over the confrontation, then reinterpret the exact message lines versus later rebuke lines to test whether the text's purpose was meeting setup/luring or rebuke.

steps.purpose: Get speaker-attributed transcript over the full confrontation so the message exchange and later rebuke can be separated by speaker and time.

expected_outputs.transcripts: speaker-attributed transcript covering the message mention, meeting setup, cliff/jump exchange, and later rebuke

steps.purpose: Determine from the speaker-attributed transcript whether the text's purpose is explicitly grounded as rebuke, breakup, wedding interference, maid-of-honor avoidance, or only as arranging/luring the brunette to the cliff meeting.

inputs.query: Using only the transcript evidence, identify: (1) who asks about the message, (2) what exact lines show what information the message conveyed or accomplished, (3) whether any line explicitly says the text itself was sent to rebuke, break up, stall a wedding, avoid maid-of-honor duties, or lure the brunette to the cliffs, and (4) which option is best supported with a careful distinction between the purpose of the text and insults/rebukes spoken later at the meeting. Quote the decisive lines and explain why option B is weaker than option D if D is chosen; if the transcript only supports meeting setup rather than explicit lure-to-cliffs motive, say that clearly before mapping to the closest option.

expected_outputs.answer: best-supported option with explicit caveat if evidence only shows meeting setup
expected_outputs.supporting_points: quoted discriminator lines separating message purpose from later rebuke
expected_outputs.analysis: reasoned option elimination grounded in transcript chronology

refinement_instructions: Preserve the existing useful anchors: the message mention near 53.474-79.495, the cliff/jump exchange near 81.352-110.883, and the later rebuke near 115.777-143.468. Replace unsupported prior claims that directly asserted 'lure to the cliffs' unless the new speaker-attributed transcript explicitly supports that stronger wording. In the trace, separate three layers: (a) the text caused or arranged the meeting, (b) the meeting occurs at the cliffs in a suicide/jump context, and (c) the harsh rebuke happens later during the confrontation. Use quoted transcript lines with timestamps/speaker roles to show why option B is weaker if the rebuke is spoken after arrival rather than being the purpose of the text. If the new evidence still does not explicitly state the cliff destination was the text's intended lure, acknowledge that the option mapping is by closest supported function rather than direct statement.
